title: Time Travel Without a Machine!
story: People dress up like they're from different time periods and pretend to be time travelers who just arrived on December 8! Some wear medieval costumes and talk in old-fashioned language, while others dress like futuristic space explorers with shiny clothes and gadgets. Time travelers might "accidentally" use modern slang in ancient Rome or act confused by smartphones while claiming they're from the year 3000. Social media fills with photos of people in costume, sharing stories about their imaginary time travel adventures. It's a day when everyone gets to be an actor and historian rolled into one silly, creative celebration!
activity_question: What do people wear when celebrating Pretend to be a Time Traveler Day?
follow_up_question: If you could time travel to any period in history, when would you visit and why?

title: Brownie Points for Everyone!
story: Chocolate lovers unite every December 8 for National Brownie Day! People celebrate by baking their favorite brownie recipes, from fudgy classics to ones packed with nuts, caramel, or marshmallows. Bakeries offer special deals, families host brownie-baking contests, and social media fills with photos of gooey, chocolatey squares. Some people try crazy new flavors like peanut butter swirl or mint chip. Others stick to grandma's secret recipe that's been passed down for years. The best part? Everyone agrees that licking the bowl is totally allowed on this sweet holiday!
activity_question: What do people often do with the brownie bowl on National Brownie Day?
bonus_fact: The first brownie was accidentally created when a baker forgot to add baking powder to chocolate cake batter!
